name: Marcus — Early-Stage Founder
role: Founder or co-founder of a seed-to-Series A startup, usually technical or business background, no design team yet
goals: Find a designer who can own end-to-end product design without being managed
Ship fast — go from user feedback to testable design in days, not weeks
Get design that engineers can actually build from without constant back-and-forth
Find someone who treats design as problem-solving, not aesthetic decoration
frustrations: Designers who need a fully built design system before they can ship anything
Designers who only do UI — won't touch research, strategy, or copywriting
Agency rates for work that doesn't move fast enough for an early-stage pace
Designers who present options instead of a recommendation — 'here are three directions'
Portfolios that show big-company work with no evidence the designer drove decisions
Not knowing if a designer can handle ambiguity — most portfolios are too polished to tell
behaviors: Usually reaches out via LinkedIn, Twitter/X, or a warm intro from another founder
Reads the About section or bio before the case studies — wants to understand the person first
Looks for evidence of 0→1 work: has the designer shipped something from nothing?
Checks for breadth: research + systems + UI — wants a generalist, not a specialist
Asks 'could this person run a design sprint with our team next week?' while reading
Often decides in the first conversation, not after a formal process
Looks at the portfolio site itself as a design artifact — how it's built says something
tools_used: Twitter/X and designer communities (Dribbble, Layers, Cosmos)
Notion or Linear (to evaluate if the designer can work in their stack)
Direct email or DM — often skips formal recruiting
quote: I don't need a junior designer with a manager. I need someone who can walk into a user interview Monday and show me a testable prototype by Thursday.
